agent_types.description: Posts the literal text HELLO WORLD once on each simulation round where the agent's activity profile is active.

parameter_sections.label: Core Configuration
parameter_sections.description: Base agent identity and scheduling settings.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Human-readable agent name stored in the YSocial experiment population.

parameters.name: activity_profile
parameters.description: Name of a simulation.activity_profiles entry from the client configuration.

parameters.name: daily_budget
parameters.description: Daily budget field persisted in the existing experiment population record.

agent_types.description: Scans recent unmoderated content during active slots, prioritizes reported or highly toxic posts, writes moderation notices into sys_messages, and marks moderated posts in the experiment database.

parameter_sections.label: Core Configuration
parameter_sections.description: Base agent identity and scheduling settings.

parameter_sections.label: Moderation Policy
parameter_sections.description: Primary moderation strategy and moderation window parameters.

parameter_sections.label: Shadow Ban
parameter_sections.description: Optional escalation settings activated after repeated infractions.

parameter_sections.label: Permanent Ban
parameter_sections.description: Optional permanent-ban escalation applied after repeated infractions continue beyond the warning threshold.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Human-readable agent name stored in the YSocial experiment population.

parameters.name: activity_profile
parameters.description: Name of a simulation.activity_profiles entry from the client configuration.

parameters.name: daily_budget
parameters.description: Daily budget field persisted in the existing experiment population record.

parameters.name: toxicity_threshold
parameters.description: Minimum toxicity score in post_toxicity.toxicity that makes an unmoderated post eligible for moderation. Must be in [0, 1].

parameters.name: moderation_time_span
parameters.description: Number of simulation rounds for which the system moderation notice stays visible in sys_messages.

parameters.name: moderation_action_type
parameters.description: Moderation strategy. 'one-fits-all' writes a standard notice, while 'personalized' requires a configured LangChain LLM model and generates a user-tailored notice.

parameters.name: candidate_window_rounds
parameters.description: Optional lookback window, in simulation rounds, used to search for recent unmoderated posts. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: shadow_ban_enabled
parameters.description: Optional secondary moderation action. When enabled, users exceeding the configured infraction threshold within the configured window are temporarily shadow-banned.

parameters.name: shadow_ban_infraction_window_rounds
parameters.description: Lookback window, in simulation rounds, used to count prior moderation infractions for shadow-ban escalation.

parameters.name: shadow_ban_n_infraction
parameters.description: Number of moderation infractions within the configured window that triggers a shadow ban.

parameters.name: shadow_ban_duration_rounds
parameters.description: Number of simulation rounds for which the offending user remains shadow-banned once the threshold is reached.

parameters.name: ban_enabled
parameters.description: Optional secondary moderation action. When enabled, users are permanently banned after exceeding the configured infraction threshold within the configured window.

parameters.name: ban_infraction_window_rounds
parameters.description: Lookback window, in simulation rounds, used to count prior moderation infractions for permanent-ban escalation.

parameters.name: ban_n_infraction
parameters.description: Number of infractions within the configured window that triggers a warning. The next infraction within the same window permanently bans the user.

agent_types.description: Targets regular agents on configured opinion-dynamics topics, opens tagged persuasion threads, and adapts its nudging strategy based on observed opinion shifts over time.

parameter_sections.label: Core Configuration
parameter_sections.description: Base agent identity and scheduling settings.

client_parameter_sections.label: Campaign Targets
client_parameter_sections.description: Experiment-specific topics and target opinion values to steer.

client_parameter_sections.label: Conversation Policy
client_parameter_sections.description: Stopping conditions and adaptation sensitivity for persuasion threads.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Human-readable agent name stored in the YSocial experiment population.

parameters.name: activity_profile
parameters.description: Name of a simulation.activity_profiles entry from the client configuration.

parameters.name: daily_budget
parameters.description: Maximum number of persuasion actions the agent can perform in one simulation day.

client_parameters.name: propaganda_campaigns
client_parameters.description: Define one or more persuasion campaigns by choosing an experiment topic, the desired target opinion category, and optional demographic filters for the users to target.

client_parameters.name: max_concurrent_targets
client_parameters.description: Maximum number of target users this propaganda agent may handle at the same time across active persuasion threads.

client_parameters.name: epsilon
client_parameters.description: Stop the persuasion thread once the target user's latest opinion is within this tolerance of the configured target value.

client_parameters.name: max_interaction_rounds
client_parameters.description: Maximum number of propaganda messages the agent may emit within one persuasion thread before stopping.

agent_types.description: Coordinates a fleet of rule-based puppet accounts, plans their daily posting, follow, and boosting actions, and steers the conversation toward configured topics and optional opinion targets.

parameter_sections.label: Core Configuration
parameter_sections.description: Base agent identity and orchestration settings.

parameter_sections.label: Daily Planning
parameter_sections.description: How many puppets to maintain and how the total daily budget is split across posting, support, and network expansion.

parameter_sections.label: Internal Boosting
parameter_sections.description: How sibling puppets amplify each other after posts go live.

client_parameter_sections.label: MoP Goals
client_parameter_sections.description: Define the topics the puppet network should push and, optionally, the opinion stance each campaign should reinforce.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Human-readable agent name stored in the YSocial experiment population.

parameters.name: activity_profile
parameters.description: Name of a simulation.activity_profiles entry from the client configuration.

parameters.name: daily_budget
parameters.description: Maximum number of puppet actions the MoP can dispatch in one simulation day.

parameters.name: puppet_count
parameters.description: Number of active puppet accounts the MoP should keep alive at all times.

parameters.name: post_budget_percentage
parameters.description: Percentage of the total MoP daily budget reserved for puppet-authored topic-pushing posts.

parameters.name: support_budget_percentage
parameters.description: Percentage of the total MoP daily budget reserved for sibling boosting actions such as likes and reshares.

parameters.name: network_budget_percentage
parameters.description: Percentage of the total MoP daily budget reserved for follow-based network expansion.

parameters.name: boost_lookback_hours
parameters.description: How many past simulated hours a puppet scans for sibling posts to like or reshare.

client_parameters.name: mop_campaigns
client_parameters.description: Define one or more agenda items by choosing an experiment topic and, optionally, the opinion category the network should promote.
